title: Public Wi-Fi Risk
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can avoiding public Wi-Fi for financial activities actually increase your transaction flexibility rather than restrict it?
What personal protocols help you maintain financial security without creating unnecessary anxiety about legitimate mobile data usage?

stages.prompt: Is it safe to use banking apps on public Wi-Fi?

options.label: Yes
options.reflection: Public Wi-Fi networks lack security encryption and create significant vulnerability to data interception and financial information theft.

options.label: Sometimes with precautions
options.reflection: Even with precautions, public Wi-Fi fundamentally lacks the security infrastructure needed to protect sensitive financial transactions adequately.

options.label: No, it risks data theft
options.reflection: Exactly! Public networks are prime targets for hackers who can intercept sensitive financial data transmitted over unsecured connections.

options.label: Only with password protection
options.reflection: Password protection secures network access but doesn't encrypt data transmission - financial information remains vulnerable to interception.

stages.prompt: What makes public Wi-Fi particularly dangerous?

options.label: Many people using the same network
options.reflection: Crowded networks increase exposure but the fundamental danger lies in lacking encryption and authentication security measures.

options.label: Lack of encryption and security
options.reflection: Perfect! Public Wi-Fi typically lacks end-to-end encryption, allowing hackers to intercept and steal transmitted financial data.

options.label: Slower connection speeds
options.reflection: Speed issues affect performance but don't create the security vulnerabilities that make public networks dangerous for financial activities.

options.label: Being free to use
options.reflection: Cost doesn't determine security - the danger comes from inadequate protection mechanisms rather than the pricing model of the network.

stages.prompt: How do hackers exploit public Wi-Fi?

options.label: Guessing passwords
options.reflection: Modern banking uses multi-factor authentication that goes beyond simple password guessing on public networks.

options.label: Intercepting data transmission
options.reflection: Exactly! Hackers create fake networks or use packet sniffing tools to capture sensitive information transmitted over public connections.

options.label: Installing malware remotely
options.reflection: Remote malware installation requires different attack vectors - public Wi-Fi primarily enables data interception during transmission.

options.label: Sending phishing emails
options.reflection: Phishing attacks are delivery method independent - public Wi-Fi specifically enables real-time data capture during legitimate transactions.

stages.prompt: What's the safest alternative to public Wi-Fi banking?

options.label: Connect through VPN service
options.reflection: VPNs add protection but introduce additional complexity and potential vulnerabilities - direct mobile data is simpler and more reliable.

options.label: Wait until home network
options.reflection: Delaying transactions creates inconvenience and may miss time-sensitive opportunities - secure mobile data enables immediate safe transactions.

options.label: Proceed with extra caution
options.reflection: Extra caution can't overcome fundamental security gaps - the network infrastructure itself lacks necessary protections for financial data.

options.label: Use mobile data network
options.reflection: Perfect! Mobile networks provide encrypted, authenticated connections that are significantly more secure than public Wi-Fi for financial activities.

stages.prompt: What should you do if you must use public Wi-Fi?

options.label: Complete transactions quickly
options.reflection: Speed doesn't reduce risk - even brief exposure on unsecured networks creates vulnerability windows for data interception.

options.label: Use only trusted apps
options.reflection: App trustworthiness matters but doesn't compensate for network-level security deficiencies that enable data capture regardless of app quality.

options.label: Avoid financial activities completely
options.reflection: Exactly! When public Wi-Fi is unavoidable, postponing financial transactions eliminates exposure to interception risks entirely.

options.label: Log out immediately after
options.reflection: Logging out protects against session hijacking but doesn't prevent real-time data interception during the actual transaction process.
